title: JUDGE STONE
author: Viola Davis and James Patterson
description: Judge Mary Stone oversees an ethically complex case in her courtroom in Union Springs, Ala.

title: THEO OF GOLDEN
author: Allen Levi
description: A man travels to a small Southern town, where he purchases pencil drawings of local residents and exchanges them for stories.

title: PROJECT HAIL MARY
author: Andy Weir
description: Ryland Grace awakes from a long sleep alone and far from home, and the fate of humanity rests on his shoulders; the basis of the movie.

title: THE WINGS THAT BIND
author: Briar Boleyn
description: The third book in the Bloodwing Academy series. A second dragon awakens and a new headmaster arrives.

title: THE CORRESPONDENT
author: Virginia Evans
description: Letters from someone she used to know push Sybil Van Antwerp toward revisiting her past and finding a way to forgive.

title: KIDS, WAIT TILL YOU HEAR THIS!
author: Liza Minnelli, as told to Michael Feinstein, with Josh Getlin and Heidi Evans
description: The EGOT icon shares some of the highs and lows of her life and career.

title: GETTING NAKED
author: Valerie Bertinelli
description: The author of “Enough Already” recounts difficult moments in her life, and her work toward self-acceptance.

title: STRIPPED DOWN
author: Bunnie Xo
description: The host of the “Dumb Blonde Podcast” shares how redemption was an important part of her journey toward reaching her goals.

title: NOBODY'S GIRL
author: Virginia Roberts Giuffre
description: The late activist and advocate for sex-trafficking survivors describes her time with Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell.

title: YOU WITH THE SAD EYES
author: Christina Applegate
description: The Emmy Award-winning actress, who received a multiple sclerosis diagnosis in 2021, shares personal stories.

title: WONDER
author: R.J. Palacio
description: A boy with a facial deformity starts school. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: THE COMPLETE COOKBOOK FOR YOUNG CHEFS
author: America's Test Kitchen Kids
description: Over 100 kid-tested recipes from America's Test Kitchen. (Ages 8 and up)

title: THE COURT OF THE DEAD
author: Rick Riordan and Mark Oshiro
description: Nico, Will and Hazel delve into the mysterious disappearances of monsters at Camp Jupiter. (Ages 10 to 14)

title: POCKET BEAR
author: Katherine Applegate. Illustrated Charles Santoso
description: Zephyrina brings a rare stuffed bear back to the Second Chances Home for the Tossed and Treasured. (Ages 8 to 12)

title: GROWING HOME
author: Beth Ferry. Illustrated Terry Fan and Eric Fan
description: Talking plants, a grumpy goldfish and an inquisitive spider save the Tupper family from utter ruin. (Ages 6 to 9)

title: HOW TO CATCH THE EASTER BUNNY
author: Adam Wallace and Andy Elkerton
description: The Easter Bunny avoids traps in order to deliver eggs and candy. (Ages 4 to 8)

title: BETTER THAN A TOUCHDOWN
author: Jalen Hurts. Illustrated Nneka Myers
description: A community comes together to turn a vacant lot into a football field. (Ages 4 to 8)

title: HOW TO CATCH A LEPRECHAUN
author: Adam Wallace. Illustrated Andy Elkerton
description: This is the year you'll finally catch a leprechaun. (Ages 4 to 10)

title: MOTHER BRUCE
author: Ryan T. Higgins
description: Bruce the Bear acquires a flock of goslings. (Ages 3 to 5)

title: FOR THE FANS!
author: Angela Song
description: Meet the members of the K-pop group Huntrix. (Ages 2 to 5)

title: DIARY OF A WIMPY KID
author: Jeff Kinney. Illustrated Jeff Kinney
description: The travails and challenges of adolescence. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: WINGS OF FIRE
author: Tui T. Sutherland
description: Only the five dragonets of destiny can unite the seven warring dragon tribes. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: THE HUNGER GAMES
author: Suzanne Collins
description: In a dystopia, a girl fights for survival on live TV. (Ages 12 and up)

title: PERCY JACKSON & THE OLYMPIANS
author: Rick Riordan
description: A boy battles mythological monsters. (Ages 9 to 12)

title: HARRY POTTER
author: J.K. Rowling
description: A wizard hones his conjuring skills in the service of fighting evil. (Ages 10 and up)

title: FAKE SKATING
author: Lynn Painter
description: After years of separation, the childhood friends Alec and Dani reunite and face their true feelings toward each other. (Ages 14 and up)

title: THE SUN AND THE STARMAKER
author: Rachel Griffin
description: The Starmaker senses a powerful magic within Aurora Finch and requests that she come study under his tutelage. (Ages 14 to 18)

title: DIVINE RIVALS
author: Rebecca Ross
description: Two young rival journalists find love through a magical connection. (Ages 13 to 18)

title: THE BOOK THIEF
author: Markus Zusak
description: A girl saves books from Nazi burning and shares them with a Jewish man in hiding. (Ages 12 and up)

title: THE RUINS BENEATH US
author: Sasha E. Sloan
description: When an Elven girl saves a prince in the woods, she is asked to become the apothecary of his kingdom. (Ages 14 and up)
